rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Celestine,

I know this has been a frustrating few weeks, and I really appreciate you sending all those photos and forwarding both order confirmations. I've been going through everything carefully so I can give you a clear answer.

For order R-8801, delivered April 18 and reported April 22, the cracked mixing bowl set qualifies for a partial refund of $29.40 because it was reported within the 5-day damage window. The silicone tray set and dish rack arrived undamaged per your photos, so those are not covered.

For order R-8854, delivered May 1 and reported May 3, the cracked blender lid was reported outside the 5-day window. I can send one replacement blender lid at no cost as a goodwill gesture, but I cannot process a cash refund for the lid.

To move forward, please send your current shipping address by May 14 and confirm you're okay with the $29.40 partial refund for R-8801. Once I have both, I'll queue the refund and replacement shipment. If the $29.40 doesn't show up within five business days after processing, please reach back out to me directly.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not change the $59.00 credit amount or the $30.00 post-credit balance.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Bertrand,

I've reviewed your billing dispute and want to give you a clear picture, as three invoices are involved.

Invoice INV-2210 for $148.00 covers March 1–31, when you were on the Standard plan for the full month. Since the service was used for the entire billing period with no mid-month changes, I cannot issue a refund for this invoice.

Invoice INV-2291 for $148.00 covers April 1–30. You downgraded on April 1, but the system charged Standard instead of the Basic rate of $89.00 because the plan switch didn't propagate before the invoice ran. This is a billing error, so I can issue a $59.00 credit—the difference between the Standard and Basic rates. If you confirm by June 5, I'll apply that credit to your next invoice, INV-2361.

Invoice INV-2361 is the current open invoice for $89.00, covering May 1–31, and is correct. After the $59.00 credit, the balance would be $30.00. Without your confirmation by June 5, the credit window closes and the full $89.00 remains due.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not include custom dashboard integration in any of the three options.
Do not change the seat price, the API flat fee, or the dedicated onboarding fee.
Do not extend the June 20 expiry date.
Do not offer additional work or scope that is not listed in the three options.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Ingrid,

Thank you for the time on Friday and for the detailed notes your team sent over the weekend. I want to make sure quote Q-5544 is easy to compare against the two add-on options I mentioned, so I am laying everything out in one email.

The base quote Q-5544 is 20 seats at $55 per seat per month, which includes the core platform, onboarding, and standard email support. This quote expires on June 20. The total at this tier is $1,100 per month.

Add-on A is the API access package. If your team adds API access, the seat price stays the same at $55 per seat per month but there is an additional flat fee of $300 per month for API access. That brings the monthly total to $1,400.

Add-on B is the dedicated onboarding program, which is a one-time fee of $1,800 and covers four structured onboarding sessions. The dedicated onboarding is not included in the base quote or Add-on A. If you add both API access and dedicated onboarding, the monthly recurring cost is $1,400 plus the one-time $1,800.

One thing I want to be clear about: the custom dashboard integration your team mentioned on Friday is not in any of these three options. That would require a separate scoping conversation and a separate quote. I cannot include it in Q-5544 or the add-ons without a new approval.

All three options expire on June 20. If you would like to go with one of the options as described, please reply by June 20 and I can send the order form the same day. If you need an extension or want to add the custom dashboard scope, let me know and I can start that process separately.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not say students entering grade 3 in the fall are eligible.
Do not say partial payment is accepted or that a spot can be held without full payment.
Do not extend or soften the June 13 deadline.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hello Ridgemont families,

We’re excited to share details about the Summer Enrichment Program for the 2026 summer session. The program runs July 7 through July 25 on the Ridgemont East campus, Room 104, and is open to students currently enrolled in grades 3 through 6 at Ridgemont. Students entering grade 3 in the fall are not eligible this session.

We can accept up to 24 students. If we get more than 24 completed applications, we’ll start a waitlist in the order applications arrive. Being on last year’s interest list doesn’t guarantee a spot—every family needs to submit a new application this year.

The program fee is $185 per student, due with the application. Applications and payment must reach the main office by Friday, June 13 at 4 p.m. The application form is Form SEP-2026, available at the main office window or on the school website. We can’t accept partial payment or hold a spot without payment.

If a student is waitlisted, we’ll hold the $185 until a spot opens or the deadline passes, then return it if no spot opens. Questions? Email enrichment@example.com or stop by the main office Tuesday through Thursday, 8 a.m. to 3 p.m.

Best,

rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Vivienne,

I looked into your order ORD-29447 and the return you started on June 2. Since each item is at a different stage, I want to make sure nothing gets lost.

- Ceramic pour-over carafe (SKU CPV-11): The return label was emailed on June 3. We haven't received the package yet. Once it arrives, I can process a $34.00 refund to your card within 3-5 business days.
- Stainless travel cup (SKU STC-07): The package arrived on June 5 and we confirmed the item is undamaged and unused. The $22.50 refund was issued on June 6 and should appear on your statement within 2 business days.
- Replacement lid (SKU LID-04): You noted the lid was cracked on arrival. I need one photo of the cracked lid before I can send a replacement. Please send the photo by June 10 at noon or the replacement request will close automatically.

I can't issue a refund for the pour-over carafe until we physically receive the item, and I can't send the replacement lid before the photo arrives. The rest is moving along.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not interpret or describe the contents of the imaging records.
Do not say the release has already been processed or is in progress.
Do not accept a paper form photo as an alternative to the portal submission.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Gwendolyn,

I'm following up on your request to send your imaging records from your April 18 appointment to Dr. Okafor's office. We can't release records without your signed authorization, so here's what to do next.

Log in to the patient portal at portal.example.org and go to "My Records" > "Release Authorization." Fill out the Release of Medical Records form, select Dr. Okafor's practice as the recipient, and check the box for "Diagnostic Imaging" under record type. Sign and submit the form inside the portal—please don't email a photo of a paper form, as our records team only accepts the portal version.

Once we receive the signed form, our records team typically processes release requests within 5 business days. If you haven't heard anything within 7 business days of submitting, call the records office at ext. 3114.

I can't give you a status or contact Dr. Okafor's office until the signed authorization is in our system. If this is urgent, please ask Dr. Okafor's office to mark the incoming request as urgent and fax a priority cover sheet to 555-0192.

Best,

rows.RewrittenText:
Dear Ingrid,

On May 15 during independent reading, Soren pushed another student's book off the desk. When I asked him to return to his seat, he said he did not care and turned away. I'm sharing this so we can figure out support together—I'm not labeling him or suggesting anything is wrong.

Since then, I've checked in with Soren each morning at arrival, and he's been calm and engaged. I've also put his name on Ms. Vega's list for a voluntary check-in. Ms. Vega is our school counselor who works with students in these situations, and this is not a disciplinary step.

Can we meet to talk? I have May 22 after 3:30 p.m. or May 24 between 9 and 10 a.m. Let me know which works, and we'll confirm by end of day May 21.

Best,

rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Octavia,

Thank you for the call on May 16 and for sharing the feature request summary for Kessler Group. I want to give you an honest update so expectations stay aligned.

The two features—bulk role assignment via CSV and a dedicated audit log export API—are on the product roadmap, but they are not committed to a specific release date. I cannot say they will be ready by your contract renewal on September 1 because our product team has not confirmed a timeline yet. Your current contract (account ID KG-00412) runs through August 31, with renewal at $3,400 per month for the 40-seat Enterprise plan.

On May 17, I submitted a formal feature priority request (reference FPR-2241) so Kessler Group's use case is documented in the product backlog. I can also connect you with our enterprise support team for a deeper roadmap conversation. If you'd like that, please reply and I'll set up an intro by the end of this week.

I cannot commit to custom development or a discounted renewal to close this gap, but if either feature lands before renewal, I will notify you immediately.

Best,
